name: Horn of Africa
climate: Arid to semi-arid lowlands; tropical highland in Ethiopian plateau; hot and humid along Red Sea and Indian Ocean coasts; bimodal rainfall in equatorial south

neighbors.name: Red Sea
neighbors.relevance: Western and northern coastline; Bab el-Mandeb strait controls Red Sea's southern entrance

neighbors.name: Gulf Cooperation Council
neighbors.relevance: GCC states operate military bases in Djibouti and Eritrea; major source of investment and labor remittances

neighbors.name: Sahel Belt
neighbors.relevance: Western neighbor; shared climate vulnerability, conflict spillover, and displacement corridors via Sudan and Chad

neighbors.name: East African Community
neighbors.relevance: Kenya and South Sudan are EAC members; regional trade integration and port access

neighbors.name: Suez Canal
neighbors.relevance: Horn of Africa security directly affects Suez Canal traffic volume via Red Sea approaches

neighbors.name: African Union
neighbors.relevance: AU headquarters in Addis Ababa; Horn conflicts are primary focus of AU peace operations

links.description: Djibouti and Eritrea control Bab el-Mandeb approaches; Horn instability disrupts Red Sea shipping
Piracy and Houthi attacks originating from Horn region threaten Suez-bound trade
GCC states are top investors and hosts of Horn diaspora labor; UAE and Saudi operate regional military bases
Djibouti is a key transshipment hub for Gulf-Africa trade; livestock exports to Gulf markets
Sudan straddles both regions; conflict and displacement flow between Horn and Sahel
China's first overseas military base is in Djibouti; BRI port and railway investments across the Horn
AU-led peacekeeping missions in Somalia and South Sudan; conflict mediation in Ethiopia and Sudan
